name: Optimize wjson performance across Lua versions

asi.notes: total_ms = sum of encode+decode across 8 benchmark scenarios (4 synthetic, 4 real datasets) for LuaJIT. Other Lua versions tracked for regression.

description: Manual escape_string for all Lua versions: LuaJIT encode -49% but PUC Lua +34-48% regression

asi.hypothesis: Replace str_gsub with manual byte scanning for string escaping
asi.result: LuaJIT encode nearly 2x faster but PUC Lua catastrophically slower
asi.key_insight: Manual byte scanning is fast on LuaJIT (JIT-compiled) but slow on PUC Lua (interpreted). str_gsub is C-optimized in PUC Lua and hard to beat.
asi.next_action: Use conditional: escape_string for LuaJIT, str_gsub for PUC Lua

description: Conditional escape_string: LuaJIT manual scanning, PUC Lua str_gsub. LuaJIT encode -55%, no PUC regression

asi.hypothesis: Conditional escape: manual byte scanning for LuaJIT, str_gsub for PUC Lua
asi.key_insight: LuaJIT JIT-compiles the manual byte scanning loop making it ~2x faster than str_gsub. PUC Lua benefits slightly from the ESCAPE_PATTERN local variable (avoids recompiling pattern each call).
asi.result: LuaJIT encode -55%, PUC Lua encode slightly better too

description: Direct integer parsing: compute from bytes instead of tonumber(str_sub). LuaJIT decode -20%

asi.hypothesis: Direct integer computation in parse_number instead of tonumber(str_sub())
asi.result: LuaJIT decode -20%, encode also improved. PUC Lua roughly unchanged.
asi.key_insight: Avoiding str_sub+tonumber for integers is a big win for LuaJIT. The JIT compiles the arithmetic loop. PUC Lua doesn't benefit much since it falls through to the same slow path for non-integers.
asi.note: The encode improvement is unexpected - might be noise or side effect of code layout

description: Move depth check to array/object branches only — worse performance

asi.hypothesis: Move depth check from decode_value entry to only array/object branches
asi.result: Worse: 214.89 vs 169.85. The extra branch instructions hurt more than the saved comparison.
asi.key_insight: A single depth > 20 check at function entry is cheaper than two conditional checks inside branches. The JIT handles the simple early-exit pattern better.
